title: OLAP Warehouse: Azure Synapse Analytics
description: Columnar data warehouse on Azure Synapse Analytics (formerly Azure SQL Data Warehouse). Provides dedicated SQL pools (provisioned MPP clusters) and serverless SQL pools (on-demand query over data lake). Dedicated pools are best for high-concurrency production workloads; serverless pools are cost-effective for ad-hoc exploration over ADLS Gen2 data. Maximum 128 concurrent queries on a dedicated pool regardless of DWU scale. Strong integration with Power BI, Azure ML, and the broader Azure data platform (Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Purview).

cost.costNotes: Serverless pool: $5/TB scanned, near-zero base cost. Dedicated pool: DW100c ~$1.20/hr (~$870/mo), DW1000c ~$12/hr (~$8,700/mo). Storage: $0.023/GB/mo on ADLS Gen2.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min ($0): serverless SQL pool with <1 TB/mo scanned fits in free exploration budget. Max ($15,000): DW2000c dedicated pool ($24/hr = ~$17,000/mo on-demand; ~$10,000/mo with 1-year reserved) + ADLS Gen2 storage + ADF pipeline runs. Most production dedicated pools land DW500c-DW1000c at $2,000-$8,000/mo reserved.
provenance.adoptionCost: ADF pipeline setup, PolyBase or COPY INTO ingestion configuration, dbt-synapse or Synapse Notebooks for ELT, Power BI DirectQuery integration. Estimate 15-20 developer days at ~$160/day = $3,000.
provenance.licenseCost: No software license. Azure pay-as-you-go. Reserved capacity provides 1-year and 3-year discounts.

provides.reasoning: Synapse dedicated SQL pools deliver MPP columnar query execution for petabyte-scale structured data warehousing.
Native Power BI DirectQuery integration with automatic dataset refresh. Also supports Tableau, Excel, and JDBC-compatible tools.
Azure Data Factory pipelines and Synapse Pipelines load data via PolyBase COPY INTO from ADLS Gen2. dbt-synapse supports in-warehouse ELT.

requires.reasoning: Synapse expects bulk loads via COPY INTO or PolyBase from ADLS Gen2. High-frequency row inserts are not supported on dedicated pools.
ADLS Gen2 is the primary staging layer for data before loading into Synapse dedicated pools or queried via serverless pools.

availability.reason: General architectural pattern applicable to any Azure OLAP workload

supports_nfr.reason: Synapse dedicated SQL pool p95 is 500ms-30s for analytical queries. Result set caching can serve repeated queries in under 100ms but cold queries have a 500ms+ floor.
Synapse dedicated pools offer 99.9% SLA. Geo-redundant backups available but active-active multi-region requires custom replication.
Synapse dedicated pool restore from geo-redundant backup takes 15-60 minutes. Pause/resume operations take 5-10 minutes.
Synapse automatic restore points are created every 8 hours with a 7-day retention. User-defined restore points can be created on demand.
Synapse dedicated SQL pool hard limit is 128 concurrent queries regardless of DWU scale. Above this, queries are queued in workload management groups.
Synapse dedicated pools are optimised for bulk COPY INTO loads. Above ~50 write QPS, ingestion should be staged via ADLS Gen2 and bulk-loaded.
Synapse supports Azure AD authentication, SQL authentication, managed identity, and SAML federation via Azure AD.
Synapse audit logs export to Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, and Azure Storage. Full query-level audit trail available.
Synapse supports column-level security, dynamic data masking, and row-level security. Compatible with PII workloads.
Compatible with GDPR when data residency controls, column masking, and right-to-erasure pipeline procedures are in place.
Compatible with HIPAA when warehouse-level encryption and appropriate BAA coverage are configured.
Compatible with CCPA when consumer data deletion triggers a pipeline reprocess that excludes deleted records.
Compatible with SOX when query audit logs provide an immutable trail of data access and transformation operations.
Durability is handled by the underlying object storage layer (S3, GCS, ADLS). All major managed warehouses provide durable storage by default.

supports_constraints.reason: Azure Synapse Analytics is an Azure-native service.
Synapse provides JDBC/ODBC drivers and is agnostic to language.
Synapse serves as analytical backend for any platform.
Synapse Analytics is selected specifically for OLAP analytical workloads.

pool_type.description: dedicated: provisioned MPP cluster, fixed cost, best for production workloads with predictable query patterns. serverless: on-demand queries over ADLS Gen2, pay-per-TB-scanned, best for ad-hoc exploration.

dw_units.description: Data Warehouse Units control compute capacity. DW500c is a reasonable starting point for production. Scale up for faster query performance; pause when idle to reduce cost.

distribution.description: hash: distribute rows by a join key column — best for fact tables with frequent joins. round-robin: even distribution — best for staging tables. replicated: copy to every node — best for small dimension tables.

ingestion_mode.description: copy-into-adls: bulk load from ADLS Gen2 (recommended, highest throughput). adf-pipeline: Azure Data Factory connectors. synapse-link: near-real-time replication from Cosmos DB or Dataverse. polybase: legacy external table approach.

result_set_cache.description: Caches results of repeated identical queries in dedicated pool storage. Subsequent identical queries return in milliseconds at no additional cost.

workload_management.description: Enables workload groups to prioritise and resource-govern concurrent queries. Recommended for production environments with mixed priority workloads.
